lifecycles._note: DNS public-domain-name + hosted-zone-record write coverage (Wave 2, NOT runtime-proven, COVERAGE-ONLY). The 6 uncovered DNS writes split into two families neither of which can be a real reversible chain on a shared account: (1) public-domain-name create is a REAL PAID DOMAIN REGISTRATION ORDER (heavy-shared-networking._note explicitly skips it) and the set/information/transfer writes act on a registered domain we don't own; (2) hosted-zone record PUT/DELETE need a hosted_zone_id under an ACTIVE private-dns (the chain the disabled networking-dns-hosted-zone lifecycle is blocked on). So every write is an OPTIONAL probe: real bodies (from data/api_bodies.json: createpublicdomainname, setpublicdomainname, setpublicdomainnamewhoisinfo, transferpublicdomainname, sethostedzonerecord) sent against builtin {unique} id segments. The engine fires each call ({unique} norm-matches the catalog '*' and is a builtin so the validator accepts it), records 400/403/404, counts coverage with no false failure. create-public-domain is NOT destructive-chained (no real registration). public-domain create returns $.public_domain.id per docs (soft-captured but unused for safety — we deliberately do not chain into a real registration). Hosted-zone record create (POST) is already covered by the disabled networking-dns-hosted-zone + heavy-shared-networking lifecycles; this fragment adds the record PUT + DELETE and the 4 public-domain writes. Covers 6 DNS WRITE ops: POST /v1/public-domain-names, PUT /v1/public-domain-names/{public_domain_id}, PUT /v1/public-domain-names/{public_domain_id}/information, POST /v1/public-domain-names/{public_domain_id}/transfer, PUT /v1/hosted-zones/{hosted_zone_id}/records/{record_id}, DELETE /v1/hosted-zones/{hosted_zone_id}/records/{record_id}.

Private DNS -> hosted zone -> A record REAL chain (NEW 2026-06-11, userguide-grounded, NOT runtime-proven — pending live validation; supersedes the disabled networking-dns-hosted-zone in scenarios.json). Encodes the now-ingested userguide flow (knowledge/formal/services/networking__dns.yaml): create private-dns (name regrpdns{ualpha} = 16 chars, rule 3-20 lowercase/digits/hyphen, ACCOUNT-GLOBAL unique across the whole platform — a 409/400 may mean the name is taken platform-wide) -> poll $.private_dns.state to ACTIVE (console machine Creating->Activing->Active; setters 400 invalid-state while CREATING — VALIDATED) -> connect the SHARED VPC via PUT connected_vpc_ids (max 5 VPCs/private-dns; a fresh create starts with NO VPC connected, and per private-dns-account-global NO activate call is needed in the creation region — activate is only for re-using the name in ANOTHER region) -> hosted zone (type private, limit 20/account; create response is a BARE envelope: id at $.id, NOT $.hosted_zone.id — api_docs response example) -> A record (ttl 3600, id at $.id; max 100 records/zone; only A/AAAA/TXT/CNAME/MX/SPF are user-settable) -> REVERSE teardown: record -> zone (private-dns delete is BLOCKED while a hosted zone exists) -> disconnect VPC -> private-dns. VPC handling: ADOPTS the session-shared VPC (adopt:vpc — connect step needs a live VPC id; fallback self-create uses the new unique 10.142.0.0/20 block per knowledge/domain-constraints.md). QUOTAS: private-dns capped at 3/account (VALIDATED) — the engine reserves the 'private-dns' budget slot on POST /v1/private-dns (path-mapped) and dependencies.json quota_kinds declares [vpc, private-dns] for this lifecycle. HEAVY because private-dns create/delete are VERY slow (hours observed in a heavy run, VALIDATED) — generous poll timeout (3600s) and this lifecycle must not be serialized behind by others (slow-provisioners-isolated flow rule). All dns writes form one optional 'pdns' group so a bad body or the platform-wide-name 409 skips only this family (cleaned up children-first). Covers DNS WRITE ops: POST /v1/private-dns, PUT /v1/private-dns/{private_dns_id} (connect+disconnect), POST /v1/hosted-zones, POST /v1/hosted-zones/{hosted_zone_id}/records, DELETE record/zone/private-dns.
